codeReview.summary: The identity syscall implementation is well-structured and correct. All six required functions (iox_getuid, iox_geteuid, iox_getgid, iox_getegid, iox_setuid, iox_setgid) are implemented with proper iOS compatibility. The implementation uses dlsym delegation to system libc with fallback to iOS defaults (UID/GID 501), and correctly returns EPERM for setuid/setgid operations since iOS apps cannot change identity. Comprehensive unit tests with 12 test cases cover consistency, error handling, and iOS-specific behavior.

issues.description: Static initialization pattern is not thread-safe. Multiple threads could race to call dlsym simultaneously. While dlsym is documented as thread-safe on most platforms, using atomic or pthread_once would be more robust.
Same thread-safety concern for iox_geteuid static initialization.
Same thread-safety concern for iox_getgid static initialization.
Same thread-safety concern for iox_getegid static initialization.
Function declarations should include brief Doxygen comments for consistency with other declarations in the header.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The static initialization pattern using 'static int initialized' is used throughout the codebase for lazy dlsym resolution. While this works, it's not thread-safe and could lead to redundant dlsym calls. Consider documenting a preferred pattern (e.g., pthread_once) in AGENTS.md for future implementations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: ixland-system/src/iox/core/iox_identity.c:28 - Pattern appears in all 4 get functions (getuid, geteuid, getgid, getegid)

sharedStateObservations.observation: The implementation correctly handles iOS-specific identity behavior by returning fixed UID/GID 501 (mobile user) and EPERM for set operations. This knowledge about iOS sandbox behavior should be captured in library documentation.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Commit message and implementation both reference iOS mobile user (501) as default

summary: PASS - The syscall-identity-impl feature is correctly implemented. All six identity syscalls (getuid, geteuid, getgid, getegid, setuid, setgid) follow the naming conventions (iox_ prefix), handle errors correctly (EPERM for set operations on iOS), and include comprehensive unit tests. Minor non-blocking issues noted around thread-safety of static initialization, but these don't prevent the feature from meeting its requirements. The commit fulfills VAL-SYSCALL-038 through VAL-SYSCALL-043 as documented in the validation contract.
